steps.title: Identify the central contrast
steps.content: The passage opens by directly contrasting two time periods: “The New York of 1847 was altogether a different city from the New York of 1909.” This contrast is the core claim, and all the details that follow explain how it changed.

steps.title: Match the contrast to the choices
steps.content:
Choice A directly restates this contrast: New York has become more developed and populated, supported by evidence of population growth and physical transformation.

The other three choices focus on peripheral or invented details—London as a comparison, voyage weather, or personal growth—rather than the city's actual transformation.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
